title: Arenas, Pools, Slabs, and Fragmentation · Object-Lifetime Lens: Pool Free List Integrity
summary: A game simulation creates millions of short-lived entities and must trade individual release for bounded phase ownership. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem pool free list integrity increment in instrumented arena and object pool. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.
Bind every arena allocation to a phase or owner whose reset cannot race with surviving borrows.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
